h2h_summary: Haiti and Scotland have never previously met in any official or friendly international fixture, making this 2026 World Cup group stage match their inaugural encounter.
team_style_home: Haiti deploy a compact 4-2-3-1 in possession that transitions to a deep 4-4-2 block out of possession, relying on disciplined defensive organization and rapid counter-attacks through the pace of Isidor and Nazon.
team_style_away: Scotland operate primarily in a 4-2-3-1 (sometimes 4-3-3) under Steve Clarke, emphasizing a high-energy engine room in central midfield, flexible pressing, and threats down both flanks and from set pieces.
wc_history_home: Haiti made their only previous World Cup appearance in 1974 in West Germany, where they did not advance past the group stage — the 2026 tournament is their historic second-ever qualification.
wc_history_away: Scotland are returning to the World Cup for the first time since 1998, their ninth overall appearance, with a record of never having advanced beyond the group stage despite coming agonizingly close on goal difference in 1974, 1978, and 1982.
group_context: Group C features five-time champions Brazil and 2022 semi-finalists Morocco alongside Scotland and Haiti, making it one of the tournament's toughest groups; for Haiti the Scotland game is likely their best chance of a positive result, while Scotland must avoid defeat to keep realistic hopes of reaching the round of 32 for the first time in their history.
strengths_home: Dangerous on quick counter-attacks with pace up front in Nazon and Isidor
Organized defensive structure that was unbeaten in CONCACAF qualifying
Experienced diaspora-based squad with top-flight European club experience
strengths_away: Midfield quality and energy with McTominay and McGinn as driving forces
Strong set-piece threat and defensive solidity under Clarke
Tournament experience and momentum from high-scoring warm-up matches
